evals.prompt: We're launching a B2B SaaS API that processes invoices. We need to define SLOs before launch. Our customers expect the API to be fast and reliable — they integrate it into their accounting workflows. We use Express.js, PostgreSQL, and deploy on AWS ECS. Help us design our SLOs.
evals.expected_output: Should identify critical user journeys (submit invoice, retrieve invoice, list invoices), define SLIs as ratios (availability, latency), set appropriate SLO targets, calculate error budgets, define error budget policies, and produce an SLO document.
assertions: Identifies critical user journeys before defining metrics
Defines SLIs as good events / total events ratios
Sets availability SLO (e.g., 99.9%) with justification
Sets latency SLO with specific threshold (e.g., 99% < 500ms)
Calculates error budget in concrete terms (minutes or requests)
Defines error budget policy with escalating actions
Does NOT set SLOs at 99.99% without justifying the cost
Recommends measuring SLIs at the load balancer, not just app-side
Produces an SLO document using the template

evals.prompt: We have a Node.js microservices architecture (5 services) and zero observability beyond console.log statements. Our CTO wants distributed tracing and proper monitoring. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend starting with OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation on one service, then expanding. Should prioritize: structured logging → metrics → traces. Should not suggest instrumenting everything at once.
assertions: Recommends starting with ONE service, not all five
Suggests OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation as the quickest win
Prioritizes structured logging first (replacing console.log)
Recommends adding trace context to logs for correlation
Suggests an OTel Collector rather than direct-to-backend export
Does NOT recommend building custom instrumentation
Provides concrete Node.js OTel setup code
Suggests a backend for receiving telemetry (Jaeger, Grafana stack, etc.)
Applies KISS — not all 5 services on day one

evals.prompt: Our on-call engineers are getting 50+ alerts per week and most of them are noise. They're burned out and missing real incidents. How do we fix our alerting?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose alert fatigue, recommend switching to SLO-based burn rate alerting, help classify alerts by severity, and suggest an alert review process.
assertions: Identifies 50+/week as severe alert fatigue
Recommends auditing existing alerts (what fires, how often, what action was taken)
Suggests removing or silencing alerts that are never actionable
Recommends burn rate alerting instead of raw threshold alerting
Distinguishes between page-worthy (urgent) and ticket-worthy (can wait) alerts
Requires every alert to link to a runbook
Suggests a monthly alert review cadence
Does NOT just say 'raise the thresholds' — addresses root cause
